steps.title: Identify confidential-containers feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your confidential-containers deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=confidential-containers
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=confidential-containers -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: [CNCF] Governance

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant confidential-containers resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
### Parts of the [CNCF onboarding issue](https://github.com/cncf/sandbox/issues/216) tracked by this issue

All governance related parts.

*This is the list of all bullet points from the [CNCF onboard

steps.title: Apply the fix for [CNCF] Governance
steps.description:
* Roles defintion
* Steering Committee initial composition
* SC scope and Responsibilities

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Confirm [CNCF] Governance is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=confidential-containers -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: * Roles defintion
* Steering Committee initial composition
* SC scope and Responsibilities. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with confidential-containers installed or the issue environment reproducible.
